text.title: Venezuela's Most Isolated Place 🇻🇪
text.u: Good morning right now we're at the base
at the bottom of Lake marakaibo the
biggest Lake in the whole country in
Puerto kancha That's the name of this
Little Town they got Blue crab factories
over there there's two there's One over
there so It's a little fishing Town
and right now aland And I who's hiding
In The Car Because it's so damn Hot
we're about to embark On A tour a
private tour up the Lake to go and spend
two Nights three days no internet no
WiFi Nothing we're going to go and sleep
on hams and go see how the
Let's go
preped the sandflies already messed me
up before but they're Not Going To get
Me Again I came prepared with What is it
where's the spray I got It
somewhere
Vamoso is be Captain for today his Roger
[Laughs]
Alejandrita here you're very hot no longer I'm
sweating a little bit
I'm sweating yoo
[Music]
you first
Well
[Music]
here put that on you don't know how to
swim It's not that it's the more professional
but
for exit and daal good Ah
[Music]
Well, quick
selfies. Why so
quiet? I feel bad, I feel bad.
Okay. See you later. Let's go, you guys. Ready? Three-day journey, two nights, the palit sleep. H and stuff.
Let's go, Che.
This is. Do
n't be shy if you want to explain
something. Perfect, bring it out from
within. So where are we now?
We're on the Concha River.
Concha is a canal that was created to allow
ships to enter, to give
Mérida access to ships. Okay, and it
was the main canal where
coffee produced in Mérida was exported
and taken to Germany. It was taken by
the Huancayo Company. They trafficked
cocoa, coffee, and some other products.
That was a long time ago, back in
colonial times. Now it's
only dedicated to fishing.
The deltas that fill Lake
Maracaibo filled
most of the canals with sediment. This is
a national park,
Juan
Manuel
26 National Park.
Wow, a family of howler monkeys in
front. Monkeys, guys,
you see
it? Look how he jumped. Ah, look at him, he's got a
big sack on his chin. This is a Box of
bone a Box of bone yeah it works as a
resonance Box for the
voice Look at
that How nice you speak
clearly how they do
Wow
uel Juan Manuel Juan Manuel Was the
Owner of exens Land in
nobody
and was of mysterious Us the and longest
and
naal
andoni son fisherman dej
s and
Everything
yeah I mean The Only way to get in here
is with a private tour there's no way
you guys know me I don't really Go with
tours and the only way to get in here is
with One so I decided Hey it's Worth it
Let take a tour um with a guide and
Everything Because I mean How the hell
Are you going to come and Explore this

incredible katum ling phenomeno with
the you
know work with an
electrical impulse yeah This is called
nodul sinal inish I don't remember the
name in English but This is an organ
that produces an Electricity and this
Electricity Keep the Heart beating Wow
That's a phenomenon that only happens
here and only happens here If We compare
the entire air with the human Body we
can Say that We are in the Heart of the
world Wow Because this
is Electricity interesting and Helping
to protect the the whole world
Because recovering the ozono layer Every
Time the catatumbo lining is strong the
layer get
Wow Oh
yeah thick lilies
here so hopefully Tonight When we sleep
in the hammars we're going to see the
Lightning phenomenon in the sky Go off
It's a Lightning show that only happens
here in this part of the world and
happens constantly Pretty much so
we'll see it

That's a New name I got a nickname for
aleandra
malandra so these palitos that you see

guards the National guards the National
guards right National guards National

they transported

and

tasty yú there Clearly, when I was

loved, I saw, and
barely everybody here sleeps, sleep.
H don't have a bed, bed. And
here, how do they get water? And that has to be
brought from somewhere else. People bring water from
there, and from the rain, from the rain, from the... fill it up.
And how does that fill up? It fills the
pipe there. Ah, okay, good, get fresh rainwater.
That's what people drink. No,
you drink rainwater. There are some who boil it,
and there are some who drink it like that.
Very, very
nice. Love. Oh, what is that? It has
many... what do I need?
What do I
need? The
weeder. Today, talking about the weeder by
Manscaped, today's sponsor. So if you guys don't want to look
like some type of
Wall Street, you want to keep those noses and
use the
weeder, I would trust this product. Up
my nose like I do with
Manscaped because Manscaped is the
world's best men's grooming
company. I've had this exact product for
over a year. Now the battery is amazing. I do
n't think I've ever charged it. Never
replace the blades; they are extremely
sharp and... Safe, not like those other
companies. Not only is the Weed Whacker a
very good product, but Manscaped has a
whole bunch of cool stuff on their
website, from boxes to ball colones to
different types of beard shavers. You can
just click the link in the description, it will
lead you to mcap.com, and you
can use my code Kurt cas, and with that
you can get 20% off any Manscaped purchase. So thank you, manate, for sponsoring this video. Speaking of which, let's get some lunch. Right, we're over here.
Try it, yeah, yeah, okay. Well, let's try it, but I'm
going to bring you mine too
so you can try it. Do you have another one, a stronger one?
Ah, well, let's try that one
then.
Petare,
the little poison, no
poison,
that's like oil, I don't
peel them. But they don't make that in Colombia, right? Of course,
on the
coast, of course. The Llanos, the
Llanos, it's
a little milder than that one, that's the
strongest, this one is more concentrated. Like
chacaro, chacaro, and that Andean one, I think
the chacaros are from...
What's the difference, like the potency,
or how this one is stronger? A little bit, no,
but in Bo Once like a piece
of
gum, look here I have another one, that's it, I

because, yes, of course, I

up,

catfish.

understand. Was that an

so check it out, guys. This is how the

is net. This is one massive net, and you
can see them pulling in the fish, they're
even jumping by themselves. Look at that! Look at that, they've got
two different types of fish
in there. Man, hard working guys, huh? They
do this in the sun all
day. And how much does a kilo of fish cost?
One kilo is a good day's
work. And some mermaids
over there, some mermaids too.

tomorrow, everybody. Now, now, it's

like, well. Where, where are we now?
Can you explain a little bit? Congo Mirador,
Congo Mirador, okay. And what was that?
What is this? This is a fishing village, a
fishing village. Many years ago,
like 200 years old, this village is more or
less. Okay, it was 200 years old. Wow, more or
less quite a lot. This village, at least,
had even a
cemetery, you know where, and they buried the
dead there. Okay, in a part that is over there. Because
of that, this is a This town,
which was very, very beautiful, still is. It's
alive now, but the sediment from Catatumbo
passed through here and fed all of this
green vegetation you see. It was all water,
water, lagoon, everything was water. But now you can't get through, there's
not much sediment. Because of that, people
migrated to other places, wave by wave.
Not many others went to Barranquita, Santa
Bárbara, and found
work in many
places. This town was very beautiful and there were many people, many people, many people,
almost all taken away. How many
people were there? About
700, more or less,
100, 700 approximately. Wow, there were already
shops, there was a medical clinic
there, there were businesses, a
school, a disco, well, there was a little
bar, no, there was a little bar with a little pool
for playing. There was
also a fire station. Oh, also. Wow,
how crazy,
why is there so much water? Well, but for
emergencies, like pregnancy, Ah, okay,
emergency. It's a very well-known town.
This town may be more well-known than
any other part of Venezuela. Wow, because
many foreigners, as well as

observe the lightning.
Many people from outside, but Yeah, yeah, nothing,

you studied there for a few

because Lake Maracaibo is
or has one of the largest oil deposits
in the world. I think the second largest here in the country, and Venezuela has the most oil in the world, which you guys probably
never imagined, right? Because
you always think when you think of oil
you think of Saudi Arabia, etc., but not
Venezuela.

silence. It looks like my cousins.
We use this for work, for
fishing. At least one fisherman
carries a radio and stays put. If his
motor breaks down, he communicates. Look, I'm in such and such a place. My
motor broke down for work. There's
no WhatsApp here. There's radio. There used to be
communication,
like,
8 years ago, that ended. So, since
cell phone communication ended, well,
we had to resort to this to
communicate with family, with the
worker, with transportation. If
possible, I call him, not directly on
his radio, but I call another one and the
other one connects me. And what? I don't know, all
the radios communicate. They all hear each other.
No, they don't hear each other, but you can't
talk to him. He can't. No, I can't talk to him, I mean,
with the radio. I can't talk
with this radio. You hear me? No. Who
is that? A
tourist? A tourist who always comes
here? Doesn't sleep? You dirty, sleeping [ __ ]!

G That's
because, yeah,
Germans, the Germans found their way over
here some back in the days, and they
mixed up, and then, yeah, through the
generations, the genetics come through, and
